tEn: Learning to summarize from human feedback
tZh: 从人类反馈中学习摘要生成
sEn: Training summarization models with human feedback significantly improves summary quality over traditional methods.
sZh: 利用人类反馈训练摘要模型，能显著提升摘要质量，超越传统方法。
absEn: As language models become more powerful, training and evaluation are increasingly bottlenecked by the data and metrics used for a particular task. For example, summarization models are often trained to predict human reference summaries and evaluated using ROUGE, but both of these metrics are rough proxies for what we really care about -- summary quality. In this work, we show that it is possible to significantly improve summary quality by training a model to optimize for human preferences. We collect a large, high-quality dataset of human comparisons between summaries, train a model to predict the human-preferred summary, and use that model as a reward function to fine-tune a summarization policy using reinforcement learning. We apply our method to a version of the TL;DR dataset of Reddit posts and find that our models significantly outperform both human reference summaries and much larger models fine-tuned with supervised learning alone. Our models also transfer to CNN/DM news articles, producing summaries nearly as good as the human reference without any news-specific fine-tuning. We conduct extensive analyses to understand our human feedback dataset and fine-tuned models We establish that our reward model generalizes to new datasets, and that optimizing our reward model results in better summaries than optimizing ROUGE according to humans. We hope the evidence from our paper motivates machine learning researchers to pay closer attention to how their training loss affects the model behavior they actually want.
absZh: 随着语言模型变得更加强大，训练和评估越来越受到特定任务数据和指标的瓶颈限制。例如，摘要模型通常被训练来预测人类参考摘要，并使用ROUGE进行评估，但这两个指标都是我们真正关心的摘要质量的粗略代理。在这项工作中，我们展示了通过训练模型优化人类偏好，可以显著提高摘要质量。我们收集了一个大规模、高质量的人类摘要比较数据集，训练了一个模型来预测人类偏好的摘要，并使用该模型作为奖励函数，通过强化学习微调摘要策略。我们将方法应用于Reddit帖子的TL;DR数据集版本，发现我们的模型显著优于人类参考摘要和仅通过监督学习微调的更大模型。我们的模型还迁移到CNN/DM新闻文章，无需任何新闻特定微调就能生成几乎与人类参考一样好的摘要。我们进行了广泛的分析以理解我们的人类反馈数据集和微调模型。我们确定奖励模型能泛化到新数据集，并且根据人类判断，优化奖励模型比优化ROUGE能产生更好的摘要。我们希望论文中的证据能激励机器学习研究者更关注他们的训练损失如何影响他们真正想要的模型行为。

contrib.en: Shows training with human feedback significantly improves summary quality over supervised learning.
contrib.zh: 表明使用人类反馈训练显著优于监督学习的摘要质量。

contrib.en: Demonstrates human feedback models generalize to new domains without task-specific fine-tuning.
contrib.zh: 证明人类反馈模型无需特定任务微调即可泛化到新领域。

contrib.en: Collects and releases a large dataset of 64,832 human summary comparisons.
contrib.zh: 收集并发布包含64,832个人类摘要比较的大型数据集。

contrib.en: Conducts extensive analysis of reward model performance and over-optimization.
contrib.zh: 对奖励模型性能和过度优化进行广泛分析。

contrib.en: Finds reward models outperform automatic metrics like ROUGE in predicting human preferences.
contrib.zh: 发现奖励模型在预测人类偏好方面优于ROUGE等自动指标。

contrib.en: Highlights the importance of aligning training objectives with desired model behavior.
contrib.zh: 强调将训练目标与期望模型行为对齐的重要性。

limits.en: High time and cost for training and data collection, e.g., 320 GPU-days for 6.7B model.
limits.zh: 训练和数据收集的时间和成本高，例如6.7B模型需320 GPU天。

limits.en: Reward model over-optimization can lead to anti-correlation with human preferences.
limits.zh: 奖励模型过度优化可能导致与人类偏好负相关。

limits.en: Models trained on Reddit TL;DR may generate biased or offensive content.
limits.zh: 在Reddit TL;DR上训练的模型可能生成有偏见或冒犯性内容。

limits.en: Summary length bias in reward models affects preference judgments.
limits.zh: 奖励模型对摘要长度的偏见影响偏好判断。

limits.en: Method requires human evaluators, limiting scalability for tasks where evaluation is difficult.
limits.zh: 方法需要人类评估者，限制了在评估困难任务上的可扩展性。

items.en: As language models become more powerful, training and evaluation are increasingly bottlenecked by the data and metrics used for a particular task. For example, summarization models are often trained to predict human reference summaries and evaluated using ROUGE, but both of these metrics are rough proxies for what we really care about—summary quality. In this work, we show that it is possible to significantly improve summary quality by training a model to optimize for human preferences. We collect a large, high-quality dataset of human comparisons between summaries, train a model to predict the human-preferred summary, and use that model as a reward function to fine-tune a summarization policy using reinforcement learning. We apply our method to a version of the TL;DR dataset of Reddit posts [63] and find that our models significantly outperform both human reference summaries and much larger models fine-tuned with supervised learning alone. Our models also transfer to CNN/DM news articles [22], producing summaries nearly as good as the human reference without any news-specific fine-tuning.111Samples from all of our models can be viewed on our website. We conduct extensive analyses to understand our human feedback dataset and fine-tuned models.222We provide inference code for our 1.3B models and baselines, as well as a model card and our human feedback dataset with over 64k summary comparisons, here. We establish that our reward model generalizes to new datasets, and that optimizing our reward model results in better summaries than optimizing ROUGE according to humans. We hope the evidence from our paper motivates machine learning researchers to pay closer attention to how their training loss affects the model behavior they actually want.
items.zh: 随着语言模型变得更加强大，训练和评估越来越受到特定任务所用数据和指标的瓶颈限制。例如，摘要模型通常被训练来预测人类参考摘要，并使用 ROUGE 进行评估，但这两个指标都只是我们真正关心的摘要质量的粗略代理。在这项工作中，我们展示了通过训练模型优化人类偏好，可以显著提高摘要质量。我们收集了一个大规模、高质量的人类摘要比较数据集，训练一个模型来预测人类偏好的摘要，并使用该模型作为奖励函数，通过强化学习微调摘要策略。我们将我们的方法应用于 Reddit 帖子的 TL;DR 数据集的一个版本 [63]，发现我们的模型显著优于人类参考摘要和仅通过监督学习微调的更大模型。我们的模型也迁移到 CNN/DM 新闻文章 [22]，在没有新闻特定微调的情况下，生成的摘要几乎与人类参考摘要一样好。我们进行了广泛的分析，以理解我们的人类反馈数据集和微调模型。我们证明了我们的奖励模型泛化到新数据集，并且根据人类判断，优化我们的奖励模型比优化 ROUGE 能产生更好的摘要。我们希望本文的证据能激励机器学习研究人员更加关注他们的训练损失如何影响他们真正想要的模型行为。

items.en: Large-scale language model pretraining has become increasingly prevalent for achieving high performance on a variety of natural language processing (NLP) tasks. When applying these models to a specific task, they are usually fine-tuned using supervised learning, often to maximize the log probability of a set of human demonstrations.
items.zh: 大规模语言模型预训练在多种自然语言处理任务上取得高性能已变得越来越普遍。当将这些模型应用于特定任务时，通常使用监督学习进行微调，通常是最大化一组人类演示的对数概率。

items.en: While this strategy has led to markedly improved performance, there is still a misalignment between this fine-tuning objective—maximizing the likelihood of human-written text—and what we care about—generating high-quality outputs as determined by humans. This misalignment has several causes: the maximum likelihood objective has no distinction between important errors (e.g. making up facts [41]) and unimportant errors (e.g. selecting the precise word from a set of synonyms); models are incentivized to place probability mass on all human demonstrations, including those that are low-quality; and distributional shift during sampling can degrade performance [56, 52]. Quality can often be improved significantly by non-uniform sampling strategies such as beam search [51], but these can lead to repetition and other undesirable artifacts [69, 23]. Optimizing for quality may be a principled approach to overcoming these problems.
items.zh: 虽然这种策略带来了显著的性能提升，但微调目标（最大化人类书写文本的可能性）与我们关心的目标（生成由人类确定的高质量输出）之间仍然存在错位。这种错位有几个原因：最大似然目标没有区分重要错误（例如编造事实 [41]）和不重要错误（例如从一组同义词中选择精确词语）；模型被激励将概率质量分配给所有人类演示，包括那些低质量的；采样过程中的分布偏移会降低性能 [56, 52]。通过非均匀采样策略（如束搜索 [51]）通常可以显著提高质量，但这些策略可能导致重复和其他不良伪影 [69, 23]。优化质量可能是克服这些问题的原则性方法。

items.en: Our goal in this paper is to advance methods for training language models on objectives that more closely capture the behavior we care about. To make short-term progress towards this goal, we focus on abstractive English text summarization, as it has a long history in the NLP community [16, 8, 54, 59, 50], and is a subjective task where we believe it is difficult to quantify summary quality without human judgments. Indeed, existing automatic metrics for evaluating summary quality, such as ROUGE [39], have received criticism for poor correlation with human judgments [55, 45, 6, 33].
items.zh: 本文的目标是推进训练语言模型的方法，使其目标更贴近我们关心的行为。为了朝着这个目标取得短期进展，我们专注于抽象式英文文本摘要，因为它在 NLP 社区有着悠久的历史 [16, 8, 54, 59, 50]，并且是一个主观任务，我们认为没有人类判断很难量化摘要质量。事实上，现有的自动评估摘要质量的指标，如 ROUGE [39]，因与人类判断的相关性差而受到批评 [55, 45, 6, 33]。

items.en: We follow the works of [3, 73], who fine-tune language models from human feedback using reward learning [35]. We first collect a dataset of human preferences between pairs of summaries, then train a reward model (RM) via supervised learning to predict the human-preferred summary. Finally, we train a policy via reinforcement learning (RL) to maximize the score given by the RM; the policy generates a token of text at each ‘time step’, and is updated using the PPO algorithm [58] based on the RM ‘reward’ given to the entire generated summary. We can then gather more human data using samples from the resulting policy, and repeat the process. We follow the works of [48, 4] and use large pretrained GPT-3 models with as many as 6.7 billion parameters.
items.zh: 我们遵循 [3, 73] 的工作，他们使用奖励学习 [35] 从人类反馈中微调语言模型。我们首先收集人类对摘要对偏好的数据集，然后通过监督学习训练一个奖励模型来预测人类偏好的摘要。最后，我们通过强化学习训练一个策略，以最大化奖励模型给出的分数；该策略在每个“时间步”生成一个文本 token，并使用 PPO 算法 [58] 根据奖励模型对整个生成摘要的“奖励”进行更新。然后，我们可以使用结果策略的样本收集更多人类数据，并重复该过程。我们遵循 [48, 4] 的工作，使用具有多达 67 亿参数的大型预训练 GPT-3 模型。

items.en: (1) We show that training with human feedback significantly outperforms very strong baselines on English summarization. When applying our methods on a version of the Reddit TL;DR dataset [63], we train policies via human feedback that produce better summaries than much larger policies trained via supervised learning. Summaries from our human feedback models are preferred by our labelers to the original human demonstrations in the dataset (see Figure 1).
items.zh: (1) 我们展示了使用人类反馈训练在英文摘要上显著优于非常强的基线。当将我们的方法应用于 Reddit TL;DR 数据集的一个版本 [63] 时，我们通过人类反馈训练的策略生成的摘要优于通过监督学习训练的更大策略。我们的人类反馈模型生成的摘要被我们的标注员认为优于数据集中的原始人类演示（见图 1）。

items.en: (2) We show human feedback models generalize much better to new domains than supervised models. Our Reddit-trained human feedback models also generate high-quality summaries of news articles on the CNN/DailyMail (CNN/DM) dataset without any news-specific fine-tuning, almost matching the quality of the dataset’s reference summaries. We perform several checks to ensure that these human preferences reflect a real quality difference: we consistently monitor agreement rates amongst labelers and researchers, and find researcher-labeler agreement rates are nearly as high as researcher-researcher agreement rates (see Section C.2), and we verify models are not merely optimizing simple metrics like length or amount of copying (see Appendices F and G.7).
items.zh: (2) 我们展示了人类反馈模型比监督模型更好地泛化到新领域。我们在 Reddit 上训练的人类反馈模型也能在 CNN/DailyMail (CNN/DM) 数据集上生成高质量的新闻文章摘要，无需任何新闻特定微调，几乎达到数据集参考摘要的质量。我们进行了多项检查以确保这些人类偏好反映了真实的质量差异：我们持续监控标注员和研究人员之间的一致性率，发现研究人员-标注员一致性率几乎与研究人员-研究人员一致性率一样高（见附录 C.2），并且我们验证模型不仅仅是在优化简单的指标，如长度或复制量（见附录 F 和 G.7）。

items.en: (3) We conduct extensive empirical analyses of our policy and reward model. We examine the impact of model and data size (Figure 4.3), study performance as we continue to optimize a given reward model (Section 4.3), and analyze reward model performance using synthetic and human-written perturbations of summaries (Section 4.3). We confirm that our reward model outperforms other metrics such as ROUGE at predicting human preferences, and that optimizing our reward model directly results in better summaries than optimizing ROUGE according to humans (Section 4.4).
items.zh: (3) 我们对我们的策略和奖励模型进行了广泛的实证分析。我们检查了模型和数据大小的影响（图 4.3），研究了在持续优化给定奖励模型时的性能（第 4.3 节），并使用合成和人工编写的摘要扰动分析了奖励模型性能（第 4.3 节）。我们确认我们的奖励模型在预测人类偏好方面优于其他指标（如 ROUGE），并且根据人类判断，直接优化我们的奖励模型比优化 ROUGE 能产生更好的摘要（第 4.4 节）。

items.en: (4) We publicly release our human feedback dataset for further research. The dataset contains 64,832 summary comparisons on the TL;DR dataset, as well as our evaluation data on both TL;DR (comparisons and Likert scores) and CNN/DM (Likert scores).
items.zh: (4) 我们公开发布了我们的人类反馈数据集以供进一步研究。该数据集包含 TL;DR 数据集上的 64,832 个摘要比较，以及我们在 TL;DR（比较和 Likert 分数）和 CNN/DM（Likert 分数）上的评估数据。

items.en: The methods we present in this paper are motivated in part by longer-term concerns about the misalignment of AI systems with what humans want them to do. When misaligned summarization models make up facts, their mistakes are fairly low-risk and easy to spot. However, as AI systems become more powerful and are given increasingly important tasks, the mistakes they make will likely become more subtle and safety-critical, making this an important area for further research.
items.zh: 本文提出的方法部分源于对 AI 系统与人类期望行为错位的长期担忧。当错位的摘要模型编造事实时，它们的错误风险较低且容易发现。然而，随着 AI 系统变得更加强大并被赋予越来越重要的任务，它们所犯的错误可能会变得更加微妙且对安全至关重要，这使得这成为一个重要的进一步研究领域。

items.en: Most directly related to our work is previous work using human feedback to train summarization models with RL [3, 73]. Bohm et al. [3] learn a reward function from a dataset of human ratings of 2.5k CNN/DM summaries, and train a policy whose summaries are preferred to a policy optimizing ROUGE. Our work is most similar to [73], who also train Transformer models [62] to optimize human feedback across a range of tasks, including summarization on the Reddit TL;DR and CNN/DM datasets. Unlike us, they train in an online manner and find the model highly extractive. They note that their labelers prefer extractive summaries and have low agreement rates with researchers. Compared to [73], we use significantly larger models, move to the batch setting for collecting human feedback, ensure high labeler-researcher agreement, and make some algorithmic modifications, such as separating the policy and value networks.
items.zh: 与我们的工作最直接相关的是之前使用人类反馈通过 RL 训练摘要模型的工作 [3, 73]。Bohm 等人 [3] 从 2.5k CNN/DM 摘要的人类评分数据集中学习一个奖励函数，并训练一个策略，其摘要优于优化 ROUGE 的策略。我们的工作与 [73] 最为相似，他们也训练 Transformer 模型 [62] 在一系列任务上优化人类反馈，包括 Reddit TL;DR 和 CNN/DM 数据集上的摘要。与我们的不同之处在于，他们以在线方式训练，并发现模型高度抽取式。他们注意到他们的标注员偏好抽取式摘要，并且与研究人员的一致性率较低。与 [73] 相比，我们使用了显著更大的模型，转向批量设置收集人类反馈，确保高标注员-研究人员一致性，并进行了一些算法修改，例如分离策略网络和价值网络。

items.en: Human feedback has also been used as a reward to train models in other domains such as dialogue [25, 68, 21], translation [32, 1], semantic parsing [34], story generation [72], review generation [7], and evidence extraction [46]. Our reward modeling approach was developed in prior work on learning to rank [40], which has been applied to ranking search results using either explicit feedback [2, 18] or implicit feedback in the form of click-through data [29, 30]. In a related line of research, human feedback has been used to train agents in simulated environments [10, 24]. There is also a rich literature on using RL to optimize automatic metrics for NLP tasks, such as ROUGE for summarization [50, 65, 45, 15, 19], BLEU for translation [50, 66, 1, 43], and other domains [61, 27, 26]. Finally, there has been extensive research on modifying architectures [22, 59] and pre-training procedures [70, 36, 49, 60, 53, 14] for improving summarization performance.
items.zh: 人类反馈也被用作其他领域的奖励来训练模型，例如对话 [25, 68, 21]、翻译 [32, 1]、语义解析 [34]、故事生成 [72]、评论生成 [7] 和证据提取 [46]。我们的奖励建模方法是在之前关于学习排序的工作 [40] 中开发的，该工作已应用于使用显式反馈 [2, 18] 或隐式反馈（如点击数据）[29, 30] 对搜索结果进行排序。在一系列相关研究中，人类反馈已被用于在模拟环境中训练智能体 [10, 24]。还有丰富的文献关于使用 RL 优化 NLP 任务的自动指标，例如摘要的 ROUGE [50, 65, 45, 15, 19]、翻译的 BLEU [50, 66, 1, 43] 以及其他领域 [61, 27, 26]。最后，还有大量关于修改架构 [22, 59] 和预训练程序 [70, 36, 49, 60, 53, 14] 以提高摘要性能的研究。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Diagram of our human feedback, reward model training, and policy training procedure.
items.capZh: 图 2：我们的人类反馈、奖励模型训练和策略训练程序示意图。

items.en: Our approach is similar to the one outlined in [73], adapted to the batch setting. We start with an initial policy that is fine-tuned via supervised learning on the desired dataset (in our case, the Reddit TL;DR summarization dataset). The process (illustrated in Figure 2) then consists of three steps that can be repeated iteratively.
items.zh: 我们的方法类似于 [73] 中概述的方法，但适应了批量设置。我们从在所需数据集（在我们的案例中是 Reddit TL;DR 摘要数据集）上通过监督学习微调的初始策略开始。该过程（如图 2 所示）包括三个步骤，可以迭代重复。

items.en: Step 1: Collect samples from existing policies and send comparisons to humans. For each Reddit post, we sample summaries from several sources including the current policy, initial policy, original reference summaries and various baselines. We send a batch of pairs of summaries to our human evaluators, who are tasked with selecting the best summary of a given Reddit post.
items.zh: 步骤 1：从现有策略收集样本并将比较发送给人类。对于每个 Reddit 帖子，我们从多个来源采样摘要，包括当前策略、初始策略、原始参考摘要和各种基线。我们将一批摘要对发送给人类评估员，他们的任务是为给定的 Reddit 帖子选择最佳摘要。

items.en: Step 2: Learn a reward model from human comparisons. Given a post and a candidate summary, we train a reward model to predict the log odds that this summary is the better one, as judged by our labelers.
items.zh: 步骤 2：从人类比较中学习奖励模型。给定一个帖子和一个候选摘要，我们训练一个奖励模型来预测该摘要更好的对数几率，由我们的标注员判断。

items.en: Step 3: Optimize a policy against the reward model. We treat the logit output of the reward model as a reward that we optimize using reinforcement learning, specifically with the PPO algorithm [58].
items.zh: 步骤 3：针对奖励模型优化策略。我们将奖励模型的 logit 输出视为奖励，使用强化学习（特别是 PPO 算法 [58]）进行优化。

items.en: We provide a more thorough description of our procedure, including details of the reward model and policy training and our quality control process, in the following sections. In practice, rather than precisely iterating this sequence of three steps, we updated our data collection and training procedures over the course of the project while accumulating labels (see Appendix C.6 for details).
items.zh: 我们在以下部分提供了更详细的程序描述，包括奖励模型和策略训练的细节以及我们的质量控制过程。在实践中，我们没有精确地迭代这三个步骤的顺序，而是在项目过程中更新了数据收集和训练程序，同时累积标签（详见附录 C.6）。

items.en: We use the TL;DR summarization dataset [63], which contains ~3 million posts from reddit.com across a variety of topics (subreddits), as well summaries of the posts written by the original poster (TL;DRs). We additionally filter this dataset (see Appendix A) to ensure quality, including using a whitelist of subreddits that are understandable to the general population. Crucially, we also filter to include only posts where the human-written summaries contain between 24 and 48 tokens, to minimize the potential effect of summary length on quality (see Section 4.1 and Appendix F). Our final filtered dataset contains 123,169 posts, and we hold out ~5% as a validation set. For the remainder of this paper, we refer to this dataset simply as TL;DR.
items.zh: 我们使用 TL;DR 摘要数据集 [63]，其中包含来自 reddit.com 各个主题（子版块）的大约 300 万条帖子，以及原始发帖人撰写的摘要（TL;DR）。我们还对该数据集进行了过滤（见附录 A）以确保质量，包括使用一个对普通人群可理解的白名单子版块。关键的是，我们还过滤只保留人类撰写的摘要包含 24 到 48 个 token 的帖子，以最小化摘要长度对质量的潜在影响（见第 4.1 节和附录 F）。我们最终过滤后的数据集包含 123,169 条帖子，并保留约 5% 作为验证集。在本文的其余部分，我们将此数据集简称为 TL;DR。

items.en: We chose the TL;DR dataset over the more commonly used CNN/DM dataset primarily because very strong performance can be attained on CNN/DM with simple extractive baselines. We find in Section 4.2 that our labelers prefer lead-3 over the CNN/DM reference summaries,444We manually check this result in Appendix E and find we generally agree with labeler ratings. and that the supervised T5 model [49] with low-temperature sampling already surpasses the reference summary quality, while copying extensively from the article. On the other hand, simple extractive baselines perform poorly on TL;DR in our human evaluations (see Appendix G.2). Instead of training on CNN/DM, we study the transfer performance of our human feedback models to CNN/DM after being trained to summarize Reddit posts.
items.zh: 我们选择 TL;DR 数据集而不是更常用的 CNN/DM 数据集，主要是因为简单的抽取式基线可以在 CNN/DM 上获得非常强的性能。我们在第 4.2 节中发现，我们的标注员偏好 lead-3 胜过 CNN/DM 参考摘要，并且监督式 T5 模型 [49] 在低温度采样下已经超越了参考摘要质量，同时大量复制文章。另一方面，在我们的人类评估中，简单的抽取式基线在 TL;DR 上表现不佳（见附录 G.2）。我们没有在 CNN/DM 上训练，而是研究了我们的人类反馈模型在训练摘要 Reddit 帖子后迁移到 CNN/DM 的性能。

items.en: We define our ground-truth task as producing a model that generates summaries fewer than 48 tokens long that are as good as possible, according to our judgments. We judge summary quality by how faithfully the summary conveys the original post to a reader who can only read the summary and not the post (see Appendix C.5 for further discussion of criteria). Since we have limited capacity to do comparisons, we hire labelers to do the comparisons for us. We rely on detailed procedures to ensure high agreement between labelers and us on the task, which we describe in the next section.
items.zh: 我们将真实任务定义为生成一个模型，该模型能生成少于48个token的摘要，并且根据我们的判断，这些摘要尽可能好。我们通过摘要向只能阅读摘要而非原文的读者传达原文的忠实程度来判断摘要质量（关于标准的进一步讨论见附录C.5）。由于我们进行对比的能力有限，我们雇佣标注员来为我们进行对比。我们依靠详细的流程来确保标注员与我们之间在任务上高度一致，这将在下一节中描述。

items.en: Previous work on fine-tuning language models from human feedback [73] reported “a mismatch between the notion of quality we wanted our model to learn, and what the humans labelers actually evaluated”, leading to model-generated summaries that were high-quality according to the labelers, but fairly low-quality according to the researchers.
items.zh: 先前关于从人类反馈中微调语言模型的工作[73]报告称，“我们希望模型学习的质量概念与人类标注员实际评估的质量概念之间存在不匹配”，导致模型生成的摘要根据标注员的标准质量很高，但根据研究人员的标准质量却相当低。

items.en: Compared to [73], we implement two changes to improve human data quality. First, we transition entirely to the offline setting, where we alternate between sending large batches of comparison data555Our decision to collect comparisons rather than Likert scores is supported by recent work, e.g. [37]. to our human labelers and re-training our models on the cumulative collected data. Second, we maintain a hands-on relationship with labelers:666We recruited labelers from a freelancing platform, Upwork, and two labeling services, Scale and Lionbridge. we on-board them with detailed instructions, answer their questions in a shared chat room, and provide regular feedback on their performance. We train all labelers to ensure high agreement with our judgments, and continuously monitor labeler-researcher agreement over the course of the project. See Appendix C.1 and C.5 for details.
items.zh: 与[73]相比，我们实施了两项改进以提高人类数据质量。首先，我们完全转向离线设置，即交替向人类标注员发送大批量对比数据555我们收集对比数据而非李克特分数的决定得到了近期工作的支持，例如[37]。并在累积收集的数据上重新训练模型。其次，我们与标注员保持密切关系：666我们从自由职业平台Upwork以及两个标注服务Scale和Lionbridge招募标注员。我们通过详细说明进行培训，在共享聊天室回答他们的问题，并定期对他们的表现提供反馈。我们培训所有标注员以确保与我们的判断高度一致，并在项目过程中持续监控标注员与研究人员之间的一致性。详见附录C.1和C.5。

items.en: As a result of our procedure, we obtained high labeler-researcher agreement: on a subset of comparison tasks, labelers agree with researchers 77% ±plus-or-minus\pm 2% of the time, while researchers agree with each other 73% ±plus-or-minus\pm 4% of the time. We provide more analysis of our human data quality in Appendix C.2.
items.zh: 通过我们的流程，我们获得了很高的标注员-研究人员一致性：在一部分对比任务中，标注员与研究人员的一致性为77% ± 2%，而研究人员之间的一致性为73% ± 4%。我们在附录C.2中提供了更多关于人类数据质量的分析。

items.en: All of our models are Transformer decoders [62] in the style of GPT-3 [47, 4]. We conduct our human feedback experiments on models with 1.3 billion (1.3B) and 6.7 billion (6.7B) parameters.
items.zh: 我们所有的模型都是GPT-3风格的Transformer解码器[62, 47, 4]。我们在1.3亿（1.3B）和67亿（6.7B）参数的模型上进行了人类反馈实验。

items.en: Similarly to [12, 47], we start with models pretrained to autoregressively predict the next token in a large text corpus. As in [48, 4], we use these models as ‘zero-shot’ baselines by padding the context with examples of high-quality summaries from the dataset. We provide details on pretraining in Appendix B, and on our zero-shot procedure in Appendix B.2.
items.zh: 与[12, 47]类似，我们从在大规模文本语料库上自回归预测下一个token的预训练模型开始。如[48, 4]所述，我们通过用数据集中高质量摘要的示例填充上下文，将这些模型用作“零样本”基线。我们在附录B中提供了预训练的细节，在附录B.2中提供了零样本流程的细节。

items.en: We next fine-tune these models via supervised learning to predict summaries from our filtered TL;DR dataset (see Appendix B for details). We use these supervised models to sample initial summaries for collecting comparisons, to initialize our policy and reward models, and as baselines for evaluation. In our final human evaluations, we use T=0 to sample from all models, as we found it performed better than higher temperatures or nucleus sampling (see Appendix B.1).
items.zh: 接下来，我们通过监督学习对这些模型进行微调，以从我们过滤后的TL;DR数据集预测摘要（详见附录B）。我们使用这些监督模型来采样初始摘要以收集对比数据，初始化我们的策略和奖励模型，并作为评估的基线。在最终的人类评估中，我们使用T=0从所有模型中采样，因为我们发现它比更高的温度或核采样表现更好（见附录B.1）。

items.en: To validate that our supervised models are indeed strong baselines for comparison, we run our supervised fine-tuning procedure with our 6.7B model on the CNN/DM dataset, and find that we achieve slightly better ROUGE scores than SOTA models [71] from mid-2019 (see Appendix G.4).
items.zh: 为了验证我们的监督模型确实是强有力的对比基线，我们在CNN/DM数据集上使用6.7B模型运行监督微调流程，发现我们实现了略优于2019年中期的SOTA模型[71]的ROUGE分数（见附录G.4）。

items.en: To train our reward models, we start from a supervised baseline, as described above, then add a randomly initialized linear head that outputs a scalar value. We train this model to predict which summary y∈{y0,y1}𝑦subscript𝑦0subscript𝑦1y\in\left\{y_{0},y_{1}\right\} is better as judged by a human, given a post x𝑥x. If the summary preferred by the human is yisubscript𝑦𝑖y_{i}, we can write the RM loss as:
items.zh: 为了训练我们的奖励模型，我们从上述监督基线开始，然后添加一个随机初始化的线性头，输出一个标量值。我们训练该模型来预测给定帖子x时，哪个摘要y∈{y0,y1}根据人类判断更好。如果人类偏好的摘要为yi，我们可以将奖励模型损失写为：

items.en: where rθ​(x,y)subscript𝑟𝜃𝑥𝑦r_{\theta}(x,y) is the scalar output of the reward model for post x𝑥x and summary y𝑦y with parameters θ𝜃\theta, and D𝐷D is the dataset of human judgments. At the end of training, we normalize the reward model outputs such that the reference summaries from our dataset achieve a mean score of 0.
items.zh: 其中rθ(x,y)是奖励模型对于帖子x和摘要y的标量输出，参数为θ，D是人类判断的数据集。训练结束时，我们对奖励模型输出进行归一化，使得数据集中参考摘要的平均得分为0。

items.en: We want to use the reward model trained above to train a policy that generates higher-quality outputs as judged by humans. We primarily do this using reinforcement learning, by treating the output of the reward model as a reward for the entire summary that we maximize with the PPO algorithm [58], where each time step is a BPE token.777Note that the reward model only gives rewards for entire summaries, and not at intermediate time steps. In RL terminology, each episode terminates when the policy outputs the EOS token, and the discount factor γ=1𝛾1\gamma=1. We initialize our policy to be the model fine-tuned on Reddit TL;DR. Importantly, we include a term in the reward that penalizes the KL divergence between the learned RL policy πϕRLsubscriptsuperscript𝜋RLitalic-ϕ\pi^{\text{RL}}_{\phi} with parameters ϕitalic-ϕ\phi and this original supervised model πSFTsuperscript𝜋SFT\pi^{\text{SFT}}, as previously done in [25]. The full reward R𝑅R can be written as:
items.zh: 我们希望使用上述训练的奖励模型来训练一个策略，该策略能生成根据人类判断更高质量的输出。我们主要通过强化学习来实现这一点，将奖励模型的输出视为整个摘要的奖励，并使用PPO算法[58]最大化该奖励，其中每个时间步是一个BPE token。777注意奖励模型仅对整个摘要给出奖励，而不在中间时间步给出。在RL术语中，每个回合在策略输出EOS token时终止，折扣因子γ=1。我们将策略初始化为在Reddit TL;DR上微调的模型。重要的是，我们在奖励中包含一项惩罚项，用于惩罚学习到的RL策略πϕRL（参数为ϕ）与原始监督模型πSFT之间的KL散度，如先前在[25]中所做。完整奖励R可以写为：

items.en: This KL term serves two purposes. First, it acts as an entropy bonus, encouraging the policy to explore and deterring it from collapsing to a single mode. Second, it ensures the policy doesn’t learn to produce outputs that are too different from those that the reward model has seen during training.
items.zh: 这个KL项有两个目的。首先，它作为熵奖励，鼓励策略探索并防止其坍缩到单一模式。其次，它确保策略不会学习生成与奖励模型在训练期间所见输出差异过大的输出。

items.en: For the PPO value function, we use a Transformer with completely separate parameters from the policy. This prevents updates to the value function from partially destroying the pretrained policy early in training (see ablation in Appendix G.1). We initialize the value function to the parameters of the reward model. In our experiments, the reward model, policy, and value function are the same size.
items.zh: 对于PPO值函数，我们使用一个与策略完全独立参数的Transformer。这可以防止值函数的更新在训练早期部分破坏预训练策略（见附录G.1中的消融实验）。我们将值函数初始化为奖励模型的参数。在我们的实验中，奖励模型、策略和值函数的大小相同。

items.en: Our main results evaluating our human feedback policies on TL;DR are shown in Figure 1. We measure policy quality as the percentage of summaries generated by that policy that humans prefer over the reference summaries in the dataset. Our policies trained with human feedback significantly outperform our supervised baselines on this metric, with our 1.3B human feedback model significantly outperforming a supervised model 10×\times its size (61% versus 43% raw preference score against reference summaries). Our 6.7B model in turn significantly outperforms our 1.3B model, suggesting that training with human feedback also benefits from scale. Additionally, both of our human feedback models are judged by humans to be superior to the human demonstrations used in the dataset.
items.zh: 我们在TL;DR上评估人类反馈策略的主要结果如图1所示。我们通过人类偏好该策略生成的摘要而非数据集中参考摘要的百分比来衡量策略质量。我们使用人类反馈训练的策略在此指标上显著优于监督基线，其中1.3B人类反馈模型显著优于其10倍大小的监督模型（相对于参考摘要的原始偏好得分为61%对43%）。我们的6.7B模型又显著优于1.3B模型，表明使用人类反馈训练也受益于规模。此外，我们的人类反馈模型都被人类判断为优于数据集中使用的人类演示。

items.en: When judging summary quality, summary length is a confounding factor. The target length of a summary is implicitly part of the summarization task; depending on the desired trade-off between conciseness and coverage, a shorter or longer summary might be better. Since our models learned to generate longer summaries, length could account for much of our quality improvements. We find that after controlling for length (Appendix F), the preference of our human feedback models vs. reference summaries drops by ~5%; even so, our 6.7B model summaries are still preferred to the reference summaries ~65% of the time.
items.zh: 在判断摘要质量时，摘要长度是一个混淆因素。摘要的目标长度隐含地是摘要任务的一部分；根据简洁性和覆盖度之间的期望权衡，较短或较长的摘要可能更好。由于我们的模型学会了生成更长的摘要，长度可能解释了大部分质量改进。我们发现，在控制长度后（附录F），我们的人类反馈模型相对于参考摘要的偏好下降约5%；即便如此，我们的6.7B模型摘要仍然在大约65%的情况下被偏好于参考摘要。

items.en: To better understand the quality of our models’ summaries compared to the reference summaries and those of our supervised baselines, we conduct an additional analysis where human labelers assess summary quality across four dimensions (or “axes”) using a 7-point Likert scale [38]. Labelers rated summaries for coverage (how much important information from the original post is covered), accuracy (to what degree the statements in the summary are stated in the post), coherence (how easy the summary is to read on its own), and overall quality.
items.zh: 为了更好地理解我们模型生成的摘要与参考摘要及监督基线摘要相比的质量，我们进行了一项额外分析，人类标注员使用7点李克特量表[38]在四个维度（或“轴”）上评估摘要质量。标注员对摘要的覆盖度（涵盖原文重要信息的程度）、准确性（摘要中的陈述在原文中陈述的程度）、连贯性（摘要独立阅读的容易程度）和整体质量进行评分。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Evaluations of four axes of summary quality on the TL;DR dataset.
items.capZh: 图3：TL;DR数据集上摘要质量的四个轴评估。

items.en: The results (Figure 3) indicate that our human feedback models outperform the supervised baselines across every dimension of quality, but particularly coverage. Although our human labelers had a high bar for giving perfect overall scores, summaries from our 6.7B PPO model achieve a 7/7 overall score 45% of the time (compared to 20% and 23% for the 6.7B supervised baseline and reference summaries, respectively).
items.zh: 结果（图3）表明，我们的人类反馈模型在质量的每个维度上都优于监督基线，尤其是在覆盖度方面。尽管我们的人类标注员对给出完美整体评分有很高的标准，但来自6.7B PPO模型的摘要中有45%获得了7/7的整体评分（相比之下，6.7B监督基线和参考摘要分别为20%和23%）。

items.en: Our human feedback models can also generate excellent summaries of CNN/DM news articles without any further training (Figure 4). Our human feedback models significantly outperform models trained via supervised learning on TL;DR and models trained only on pretraining corpora. In fact, our 6.7B human feedback model performs almost as well as a 6.7B model that was fine-tuned on the CNN/DM reference summaries, despite generating much shorter summaries.
items.zh: 我们的人类反馈模型还能在无需进一步训练的情况下，生成CNN/DM新闻文章的出色摘要（图4）。我们的人类反馈模型显著优于在TL;DR上通过监督学习训练的模型以及仅在预训练语料上训练的模型。事实上，我们的6.7B人类反馈模型的表现几乎与在CNN/DM参考摘要上微调的6.7B模型相当，尽管生成的摘要要短得多。

items.en: Since our human feedback models transferred to CNN/DM have little overlap in summary length distribution with models trained on CNN/DM, with about half as many tokens on average, they are difficult to compare directly. Thus our evaluations in Figure 4 use a 7-point Likert scale on four quality dimensions, as in Section 4.1 (see Appendix C.5 for labeler instructions). In Figure 4(b) we show the average overall score at different summary lengths, which suggests our human feedback models would perform even better if they generated longer summaries. Qualitatively, CNN/DM summaries from our human feedback models are consistently fluent and reasonable representations of the article; we show examples on our website and in Appendix H.
items.zh: 由于我们迁移到CNN/DM的人类反馈模型与在CNN/DM上训练的模型在摘要长度分布上重叠很少，平均词数约为后者的一半，因此很难直接比较。因此，我们在图4中的评估使用了7点李克特量表，涵盖四个质量维度，如第4.1节所述（标签员说明见附录C.5）。在图4(b)中，我们展示了不同摘要长度下的平均总体得分，这表明如果我们的人类反馈模型生成更长的摘要，其表现会更好。定性来看，我们人类反馈模型生成的CNN/DM摘要始终流畅且合理地代表了文章；我们在网站和附录H中展示了示例。

items.en: Optimizing against our reward model is supposed to make our policy align with human preferences. But the reward model isn’t a perfect representation of our labeler preferences, as it has limited capacity and only sees a small amount of comparison data from a relatively narrow distribution of summaries. While we can hope our reward model generalizes to summaries unseen during training, it’s unclear how much one can optimize against the reward model until it starts giving useless evaluations.
items.zh: 针对我们的奖励模型进行优化，旨在使我们的策略与人类偏好对齐。但奖励模型并非标签员偏好的完美表示，因为它容量有限，且仅从相对狭窄的摘要分布中看到了少量比较数据。虽然我们希望奖励模型能泛化到训练中未见过的摘要，但尚不清楚在奖励模型开始给出无用的评估之前，可以对其进行多大程度的优化。

items.en: To answer this question, we created a range of policies optimized against an earlier version of our reward model, with varying degrees of optimization strength, and asked labelers to compare samples from them to the reference summaries. Figure 5 shows the results for PPO at a range of KL penalty coefficients (β𝛽\beta). Under light optimization, the models improve (according to labelers). However, as we optimize further, true preferences fall off compared to the prediction, and eventually the reward model becomes anti-correlated with human preferences. Though this is clearly undesirable, we note that this over-optimization also happens with ROUGE (see [45] and Appendix G.3). Similar behavior has been observed in learned reward functions in the robotics domain [5].
items.zh: 为了回答这个问题，我们创建了一系列针对早期版本奖励模型进行不同程度优化的策略，并请标签员将它们的样本与参考摘要进行比较。图5展示了在不同KL惩罚系数（β）下PPO的结果。在轻度优化下，模型有所改进（根据标签员的判断）。然而，随着进一步优化，真实偏好相对于预测值下降，最终奖励模型与人类偏好呈负相关。虽然这显然不可取，但我们注意到这种过度优化也发生在ROUGE上（见[45]和附录G.3）。在机器人领域的奖励函数学习中也观察到了类似行为[5]。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Preference scores versus degree of reward model optimization. Optimizing against the reward model initially improves summaries, but eventually overfits, giving worse summaries. This figure uses an earlier version of our reward model (see rm3 in Appendix C.6). See Appendix H.2 for samples from the KL 250 model.
items.capZh: 图5：偏好分数与奖励模型优化程度的关系。针对奖励模型进行优化最初会改善摘要，但最终会过拟合，导致摘要变差。此图使用了早期版本的奖励模型（见附录C.6中的rm3）。KL 250模型的样本见附录H.2。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Reward model performance versus data size and model size. Doubling amount of training data leads to a ~1.1% increase in reward model validation accuracy, whereas doubling the model size leads to a ~1.8% increase. The 6.7B model trained on all data begins approaching the accuracy of a single human.
items.capZh: 图6：奖励模型性能与数据量和模型规模的关系。训练数据量翻倍导致奖励模型验证准确率提升约1.1%，而模型规模翻倍导致提升约1.8%。在所有数据上训练的6.7B模型开始接近单个人类的准确率。

items.en: We conduct an ablation to determine how data quantity and model size affect reward modeling performance. We train 7 reward models ranging from 160M to 13B parameters, on 8k to 64k human comparisons from our dataset. We find that doubling the training data amount leads to a ~1.1% increase in the reward model validation set accuracy, whereas doubling the model size leads to a ~1.8% increase (Figure 4.3).
items.zh: 我们进行了一项消融实验，以确定数据量和模型规模如何影响奖励建模性能。我们训练了7个奖励模型，参数从160M到13B不等，使用了数据集中8k到64k的人类比较数据。我们发现，训练数据量翻倍导致奖励模型验证集准确率提升约1.1%，而模型规模翻倍导致提升约1.8%（图4.3）。

items.en: We probe our reward model by evaluating it on several validation sets. We show the full results in Appendix G.6, and highlight them here. We find that our reward models generalize to evaluating CNN/DM summaries (Appendix G.7), agreeing with labeler preferences 62.4% and 66.5% of the time (for our 1.3B and 6.7B models, respectively). Our 6.7B reward model nearly matches the inter-labeler agreement value of 66.9%.
items.zh: 我们通过在多个验证集上评估来探究奖励模型。完整结果见附录G.6，此处重点介绍。我们发现奖励模型能泛化到评估CNN/DM摘要（附录G.7），与标签员偏好的一致性分别为62.4%和66.5%（针对我们的1.3B和6.7B模型）。我们的6.7B奖励模型几乎达到了标签员间一致性值66.9%。

items.en: We also find that our reward models are sensitive to small but semantically important details in the summary. We construct an additional validation set by having labelers make minimal edits to summaries to improve them. Our RMs prefer the edited summaries almost as often (79.4% for 1.3B and 82.8% for 6.7B) as a separate set of human evaluators (84.1%). Further, when comparing the reference summaries to perturbed summaries where the participants’ roles are reversed, our models reliably select the original summary (92.9% of the time for 1.3B, 97.2% for 6.7B). However, our RMs are biased towards longer summaries: our 6.7B RM prefers improving edits that make the summary shorter only 62.6% of the time (vs. 76.4% for humans).
items.zh: 我们还发现奖励模型对摘要中微小但语义重要的细节很敏感。我们构建了一个额外的验证集，让标签员对摘要进行最小编辑以改进它们。我们的奖励模型偏好编辑后摘要的频率（1.3B为79.4%，6.7B为82.8%）几乎与另一组人类评估者（84.1%）相当。此外，当比较参考摘要与参与者角色互换的扰动摘要时，我们的模型可靠地选择了原始摘要（1.3B为92.9%，6.7B为97.2%）。然而，我们的奖励模型偏向于更长的摘要：我们的6.7B奖励模型偏好使摘要变短的改进编辑仅占62.6%（而人类为76.4%）。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Summary quality as a function of metric optimized and amount of optimization, using best-of-N rejection sampling. We evaluate ROUGE, our main reward models, and an earlier iteration of the 1.3B model trained on approximately 75% as much data (see Table LABEL:tab:batch_data for details). ROUGE appears to peak both sooner and at a substantially lower preference rate than all reward models. Details in Appendix G.3.
items.capZh: 图7：摘要质量作为优化指标和优化量的函数，采用最佳N拒绝采样。我们评估了ROUGE、我们的主要奖励模型以及一个早期迭代的1.3B模型（训练数据约为75%，详见表格LABEL:tab:batch_data）。ROUGE似乎比所有奖励模型更早达到峰值，且偏好率显著更低。详情见附录G.3。

items.en: We study how well various automatic metrics act as predictors for human preferences, and compare them to our RMs. Specifically, we examine ROUGE, summary length, amount of copying from the post,888We measure copying by computing the longest common subsequence of bigrams with the original Reddit post or news article, and dividing by the number of bigrams in the summary. and log probability under our baseline supervised models. We present a full matrix of agreement rates between these metrics in Appendix G.7.
items.zh: 我们研究了各种自动指标作为人类偏好预测器的表现，并将其与我们的奖励模型进行比较。具体来说，我们检查了ROUGE、摘要长度、从帖子中复制的量（我们通过计算与原始Reddit帖子或新闻文章的最长公共二元组子序列，并除以摘要中的二元组数来衡量复制程度）以及在我们基线监督模型下的对数概率。我们在附录G.7中给出了这些指标之间一致率的完整矩阵。

items.en: We find that our learned reward models consistently outperform other metrics, even on the CNN/DM dataset on which it was never trained. We also find that ROUGE fails to track sample quality as our models improve. While ROUGE has ~57% agreement with labelers when comparing samples from our supervised baseline models, this drops to ~50% for samples from our human feedback model.
items.zh: 我们发现，我们学习的奖励模型始终优于其他指标，即使在从未训练过的CNN/DM数据集上也是如此。我们还发现，随着模型改进，ROUGE无法跟踪样本质量。虽然ROUGE在比较监督基线模型的样本时与标签员的一致性约为57%，但在比较人类反馈模型的样本时，这一比例下降到约50%。

items.en: Similarly, log probability agreement with humans drops to ≤\leq50% on comparisons between samples from our human feedback models, while our RMs still perform above chance (62%). Scaling up the size of the supervised model does not reliably improve log probability’s agreement with labelers.
items.zh: 类似地，在比较人类反馈模型的样本时，对数概率与人类的一致性下降到≤50%，而我们的奖励模型仍高于随机水平（62%）。扩大监督模型的规模并不能可靠地提高对数概率与标签员的一致性。

items.en: In Figure 7, we show that optimizing ROUGE using a simple optimization scheme doesn’t consistently increase quality, as has been noted in [45]. Optimization against ROUGE peaks both sooner and at a substantially lower quality rate than optimization against our reward models.
items.zh: 在图7中，我们展示了使用简单优化方案优化ROUGE并不能持续提高质量，正如[45]所指出的。针对ROUGE的优化比针对我们奖励模型的优化更早达到峰值，且质量率显著更低。

items.en: One limitation of our work is the time and cost required to produce our final models. Notably, fine-tuning our 6.7B model with RL required approximately 320 GPU-days. Our data collection procedure is also expensive compared to prior work — the training set took thousands of labeler hours and required significant researcher time to ensure quality. For this reason, we were unable to collect baselines such as an equivalent amount of high-quality human demonstrations for supervised baselines. See D for more discussion. We leave this ablation to future work. Nevertheless, we believe reward modeling is more likely to scale to tasks where it is extremely skill-intensive or time-consuming to provide good demonstrations.
items.zh: 我们工作的一个局限性是生成最终模型所需的时间和成本。值得注意的是，使用强化学习微调我们的6.7B模型需要大约320 GPU天。与先前工作相比，我们的数据收集过程也成本高昂——训练集花费了数千小时的标签员时间，并需要大量研究人员时间以确保质量。因此，我们无法收集基线数据，例如用于监督基线的等量高质量人类演示。更多讨论见附录D。我们将此消融实验留给未来工作。尽管如此，我们相信奖励建模更有可能扩展到那些提供良好演示极其需要技能或耗时的任务。

items.en: The methods in this paper could be applied to any task where humans can compare samples, including dialogue, machine translation, question answering, speech synthesis, and music generation. We expect this method to be particularly important for generating long samples, where the distributional shift and degeneracy of maximum likelihood samples can be problematic. It may be possible to improve sample efficiency by training to predict feedback across many tasks [42].
items.zh: 本文中的方法可应用于任何人类能比较样本的任务，包括对话、机器翻译、问答、语音合成和音乐生成。我们期望该方法对于生成长样本尤其重要，因为最大似然样本的分布偏移和退化可能成为问题。通过训练跨多个任务预测反馈，可能提高样本效率[42]。

items.en: We are particularly interested in scaling human feedback to tasks where humans can’t easily evaluate the quality of model outputs. In this setting, it is particularly challenging to identify whether an ML system is aligned with the human designer’s intentions. One approach is to train ML systems to help humans perform the evaluation task quickly and accurately [9].
items.zh: 我们特别感兴趣的是将人类反馈扩展到人类难以轻易评估模型输出质量的任务。在这种情况下，识别机器学习系统是否与人类设计者的意图对齐尤其具有挑战性。一种方法是训练机器学习系统帮助人类快速准确地执行评估任务[9]。

items.en: There is also a rich landscape of human feedback methods beyond binary comparisons that could be explored for training models [28, 17, 44, 64]. For example, we could solicit high-quality demonstrations from labelers, have labelers edit model outputs to make them better, or have labelers provide explanations for why they preferred one model output over another. All of this feedback could be leveraged as a signal to train more capable reward models and policies.
items.zh: 除了二元比较之外，还有丰富的人类反馈方法可用于训练模型[28, 17, 44, 64]。例如，我们可以向标签员征集高质量的演示，让标签员编辑模型输出以使其更好，或者让标签员解释为什么他们偏好一个模型输出而非另一个。所有这些反馈都可以作为信号，用于训练更强大的奖励模型和策略。

items.en: The techniques we explore in this paper are generic techniques that could be used in a wide variety of machine learning applications, for any task where it is feasible for humans to evaluate the quality of model outputs. Thus, the potential implications are quite broad.
items.zh: 本文探索的技术是通用技术，可广泛应用于各种机器学习应用，适用于人类能够评估模型输出质量的任何任务。因此，潜在的影响相当广泛。

items.en: Our research is primarily motivated by the potential positive effects of aligning machine learning algorithms with the designer’s preferences. Many machine learning applications optimize simple metrics which are only rough proxies for what the designer intends. This can lead to problems, such as Youtube recommendations promoting click-bait [11]. In the short term, improving techniques for learning from and optimizing human preferences directly may enable these applications to be more aligned with human well-being.
items.zh: 我们的研究主要受到将机器学习算法与设计者偏好对齐的潜在积极影响的驱动。许多机器学习应用优化简单的指标，这些指标只是设计者意图的粗略代理。这可能导致问题，例如YouTube推荐推广点击诱饵[11]。短期内，改进直接从人类偏好学习和优化的技术，可能使这些应用更符合人类福祉。

items.en: In the long term, as machine learning systems become more capable it will likely become increasingly difficult to ensure that they are behaving safely: the mistakes they make might be more difficult to spot, and the consequences will be more severe. For instance, writing an inaccurate summary of a news article is both easy to notice (one simply has to read the original article) and has fairly low consequences. On the other hand, imitating human driving may be substantially less safe than driving to optimize human preferences. We believe that the techniques we explore in this paper are promising steps towards mitigating the risks from such capable systems, and better aligning them with what humans care about.
items.zh: 从长远来看，随着机器学习系统能力增强，确保其安全行为将变得越来越困难：它们所犯的错误可能更难发现，后果也将更加严重。例如，写一篇不准确的新闻摘要很容易被发现（只需阅读原文），且后果相当轻微。另一方面，模仿人类驾驶可能远不如以优化人类偏好为目标驾驶安全。我们认为，本文探索的技术是朝着减轻此类强大系统风险、使其更好地与人类关切对齐的有前景的步骤。

items.en: Unfortunately, our techniques also enable malicious actors to more easily train models that cause societal harm. For instance, one could use human feedback to fine-tune a language model to be more persuasive and manipulate humans’ beliefs, or to induce dependence of humans on the technology, or to generate large amounts of toxic or hurtful content intended to harm specific individuals. Avoiding these outcomes is a significant challenge for which there are few obvious solutions.
items.zh: 不幸的是，我们的技术也使恶意行为者更容易训练造成社会危害的模型。例如，人们可以利用人类反馈微调语言模型，使其更具说服力并操纵人类信念，或诱导人类对技术的依赖，或生成大量旨在伤害特定个人的有毒或有害内容。避免这些后果是一项重大挑战，几乎没有显而易见的解决方案。

items.en: Large-scale models trained with human feedback could have significant impacts on many groups. Thus, it is important to be careful about how we define the ‘good’ model behavior that human labelers will reinforce. Deciding what makes a good summary is fairly straightforward, but doing this for tasks with more complex objectives, where different humans might disagree on the correct model behavior, will require significant care. In these cases, it is likely not appropriate to use researcher labels as the ‘gold standard’; rather, individuals from groups impacted by the technology should be included in the process to define ‘good’ behavior, and hired as labelers to reinforce this behavior in the model.
items.zh: 使用人类反馈训练的大规模模型可能对许多群体产生重大影响。因此，谨慎定义人类标注者将强化的“良好”模型行为至关重要。决定什么是好的摘要相当直接，但对于具有更复杂目标的任务，不同人类可能对正确的模型行为存在分歧，这需要格外小心。在这些情况下，将研究人员的标注作为“黄金标准”可能不合适；相反，应让受技术影响的群体中的个体参与定义“良好”行为的过程，并雇佣他们作为标注者来强化模型中的这种行为。

items.en: We chose to train on the Reddit TL;DR dataset because the summarization task is significantly more challenging than on CNN/DM. However, since the dataset consists of user-submitted posts with minimal moderation, they often contain content that is offensive or reflects harmful social biases. This means our models can generate biased or offensive summaries, as they have been trained to summarize such content. For this reason, we recommend that the potential harms of our models be thoroughly studied before deploying them in user-facing applications.
items.zh: 我们选择在Reddit TL;DR数据集上训练，因为摘要任务比CNN/DM更具挑战性。然而，由于该数据集由用户提交的帖子组成，且审核极少，因此通常包含冒犯性内容或反映有害社会偏见的内容。这意味着我们的模型可能生成有偏见或冒犯性的摘要，因为它们已被训练来总结此类内容。因此，我们建议在将模型部署到面向用户的应用之前，彻底研究其潜在危害。

items.en: Finally, by improving the ability of machine learning algorithms to perform tasks that were previously only achievable by humans, we are increasing the likelihood of many jobs being automated, potentially leading to significant job loss. Without suitable policies targeted at mitigating the effects of large-scale unemployment, this could also lead to significant societal harm.
items.zh: 最后，通过提高机器学习算法执行以前仅由人类完成的任务的能力，我们增加了许多工作被自动化的可能性，可能导致大量失业。如果没有旨在减轻大规模失业影响的适当政策，这也可能导致重大的社会危害。

items.en: We’d like to thank Beth Barnes for help with labeler hiring and general encouragement; Geoffrey Irving for guidance on earlier iterations of the project and inspiring conversations; Ben Mann, Tom Brown, Nick Ryder, and Melanie Subbiah for training and evaluating our pretrained models; Chris Hesse, Eric Sigler, Benjamin Chess, Christopher Berner, Clemens Winter, Mateusz Litwin, and many others for supporting us through computing infrastructure improvements and maintenance; Scott Gray for writing fast GPU kernels; Arvind Neelakantan and Wojciech Kryscinski for discussions on how to present the work, experiment design, and what datasets to use; Shan Carter for help designing the main diagram; Douwe Kiela, Zach Lipton, and Alex Irpan for providing feedback on the paper; and Gretchen Krueger for co-writing the model card accompanying the paper.
items.zh: 我们要感谢Beth Barnes在标注者招聘和一般鼓励方面的帮助；感谢Geoffrey Irving对项目早期迭代的指导和启发性的对话；感谢Ben Mann、Tom Brown、Nick Ryder和Melanie Subbiah训练和评估我们的预训练模型；感谢Chris Hesse、Eric Sigler、Benjamin Chess、Christopher Berner、Clemens Winter、Mateusz Litwin以及许多其他人在计算基础设施改进和维护方面的支持；感谢Scott Gray编写快速的GPU内核；感谢Arvind Neelakantan和Wojciech Kryscinski就如何展示工作、实验设计以及使用哪些数据集进行的讨论；感谢Shan Carter帮助设计主图；感谢Douwe Kiela、Zach Lipton和Alex Irpan对论文的反馈；以及感谢Gretchen Krueger共同撰写随论文发布的模型卡。

items.en: Finally, we’d like to thank all of our contractors for providing the data that was essential for training the models in this paper, including: Emill Jayson Caypuno, Rachelle Froyalde, Cyra Denura, Alex Malek, Isik Agil, Reshmi Patel, William Yap, Natalie Silver, Erol Akbaba, Jennifer Brillo, Alexandra Uifalean, Morris Stuttard, Russell Bernandez, Tasmai Dave, Rachel Wallace, Jenny Fletcher, Jian Ouyang, Justin Dill, Maria Orzek, Megan Niffenegger, William Sells, Emily Mariner, Andrew Seely, Lychelle Ignacio, Jelena Ostojic, Nhan Tran, Purev Batdelgar, Valentina Kezic, Michelle Wilkerson, Kelly Guerrero, Heather Scott, Sarah Mulligan, Gabriel Ricafrente, Kara Bell, Gabriel Perez, and Alfred Lee.
items.zh: 最后，我们要感谢所有为本文训练模型提供关键数据的承包商，包括：Emill Jayson Caypuno、Rachelle Froyalde、Cyra Denura、Alex Malek、Isik Agil、Reshmi Patel、William Yap、Natalie Silver、Erol Akbaba、Jennifer Brillo、Alexandra Uifalean、Morris Stuttard、Russell Bernandez、Tasmai Dave、Rachel Wallace、Jenny Fletcher、Jian Ouyang、Justin Dill、Maria Orzek、Megan Niffenegger、William Sells、Emily Mariner、Andrew Seely、Lychelle Ignacio、Jelena Ostojic、Nhan Tran、Purev Batdelgar、Valentina Kezic、Michelle Wilkerson、Kelly Guerrero、Heather Scott、Sarah Mulligan、Gabriel Ricafrente、Kara Bell、Gabriel Perez和Alfred Lee。
